case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for dimethylnitrosamine on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: Recovered from top-level results artifacts; case directory is empty and needs archival repair

plan.reasoning: No prior history. Initial plan targets O (index 4) of the nitroso group binding ontop to Ti, a chemically plausible first site for nitrosamine adsorption via lone-pair donation. Molecule type is appropriate as all atoms have closed shells.

attempt_records.history_entry: 【✅ Perfect Adsorption】 ontop @ ['Ti'] (Index [4]) -> Site: ontop (Ti) [🌟 New Best] | E=-0.920 eV

plan.reasoning: History contains only one successful ontop-Ti adsorption via O (index 4) at -0.920 eV. To search for a potentially lower-energy configuration we must propose a distinct site. The nitroso oxygen (index 4) remains the most plausible donor; we therefore target a chemically distinct Bi-Ti bridge environment on the alloy surface. This satisfies all site-naming, binding-index and Molecule-type constraints while exploring surface heterogeneity.

attempt_records.history_entry: 【✅ Perfect Adsorption】 bridge @ ['Bi', 'Ti'] (Index [4]) -> Site: bridge (Bi,Ti) | E=-0.850 eV

plan.reasoning: History shows ontop-Ti (O index 4) at -0.92 eV is best; bridge Bi-Ti (same O) at -0.85 eV is worse. All high-symmetry sites with O-binding already sampled. New plan: hollow Ti-Ti-Ti site with same O (index 4) to exploit possible surface heterogeneity. No dissociation expected. Predicted MACE-MP-0 energy slightly worse than best due to steric crowding.
